description: Replace npm search CLI with the npm registry HTTP API for plugin search (faster, no 20-result limit, pagination support), and add direct package probe to the server-side search so the sidebar finds plugins like Notion that keyword search misses.

userStories.title: Replace npm search CLI with registry HTTP API in the MCP server
userStories.description: The server's searchNpmPlugins spawns `npm search` which defaults to 20 results and cannot be reliably filtered. Replace it with a direct HTTP call to the npm registry search API (GET /-/v1/search) which is 4x faster (170ms vs 630ms), supports up to 250 results per page with pagination via `from`, and avoids spawning a child process. Also add a direct package probe (registry GET /{package}) when a query is provided, so exact-name matches like 'notion' are always found even if keyword search ranks them beyond the result window.
acceptanceCriteria: searchNpmPlugins uses fetch() to call https://registry.npmjs.org/-/v1/search instead of spawning npm search
When total results exceed 250, pagination via the `from` parameter fetches all pages
When a query is provided, a direct probe of @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-{query} is performed in parallel with the keyword search
Direct probe results appear first in the results list (exact match priority)
Results are deduplicated by package name (direct probe result wins over keyword search duplicate)
Searching 'notion' in the sidebar returns the Notion plugin
Searching with no query returns all opentabs plugins (not just the first 20)
The existing timeout and error handling is preserved

userStories.title: Replace npm search CLI with registry HTTP API in the CLI
userStories.description: The CLI's npmSearchPlugins also spawns `npm search` with the same 20-result default limit. Replace it with the registry HTTP API for consistency with the server, and to fix the same pagination issue. The CLI already has direct probe via buildDirectLookupCandidates + fetchPackageInfo using `npm view` — replace those with fetch() too for speed.
acceptanceCriteria: npmSearchPlugins uses fetch() to call the registry API instead of spawning npm search
fetchPackageInfo uses fetch() to call the registry API instead of spawning npm view
opentabs plugin search with no query returns all opentabs plugins
opentabs plugin search notion returns the Notion plugin
The existing direct probe logic (buildDirectLookupCandidates) still works for both scoped and unscoped packages
